pattern: Maintain per-ID counts in a hash map and track the current maximum count in a heap; lazy-delete stale heap entries using the current count map as ground truth.
hints: After each step you need the maximum count across all IDs. A heap can give you the max in O(log n) but removals are expensive — can you defer them?
Use a max-heap of (count, id) but never remove entries proactively; when you pop a top entry, check if it matches the current count in the map. If not, discard it and pop again.
Update the count map with each freq[i], push the new (count, id) pair onto the heap, then lazy-pop stale entries to find the true max for ans[i].

edge_inputs.description: same ID added then fully removed
two IDs alternate being most frequent
all same ID, monotonically increasing count
single step gives zero IDs then one ID
ID reaches 0 count then re-appears; heap must not return stale positive entry
multiple IDs with same max count; any is fine but result must be non-negative

stress.description: 10k steps on 100 distinct IDs to test heap correctness under many add/remove cycles
